original_hypothesis.title: CreditToken delegateBySig uses raw ecrecover without ECDSA malleability checks, potentially enabling signature replay variants in downstream tooling
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ERC20MultiVotes.delegateBySig uses raw ecrecover and only checks `signer != address(0)` and nonce match. It does not enforce EIP-2 low-s malleability constraints or v normalization beyond whatever ecrecover accepts. While nonce usage prevents direct replay, malleable signatures can create operational issues in off-chain tooling, signature validation libraries, and monitoring (multiple valid signatures for same message), and may open edge-case bypasses depending on wallet/provider behavior.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is less likely to be exploited directly on-chain for theft due to nonce, but can cause operational governance/voting inconsistencies and signature handling bugs in production pipelines.

proof_of_signal.pattern: address signer = ecrecover(digest, v, r, s); // no ECDSA.recover / no low-s check

preconditions: User signs delegation off-chain and signature is used on-chain
Attacker/tooling can craft alternate malleable form (high-s) that still recovers same signer under ecrecover rules (chain-dependent)
Downstream systems treat distinct signature bytes as distinct authorizations

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Raw ecrecover rather than OpenZeppelin ECDSA library

operational_questions: Do target chains enforce EIP-2 low-s at the protocol level for ecrecover precompile inputs?
Do relayers/indexers treat different signature encodings as separate intents?
Is delegateBySig used in governance automation where signature uniqueness matters?
validation_requirements: Test malleable signature variants against delegateBySig on target chain
Confirm whether ecrecover accepts high-s and how clients/wallets behave

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Obtain a valid delegation signature from a user (legitimate workflow)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Generate alternate malleable signature encoding (if accepted)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Use in off-chain systems to cause duplicate processing / monitoring bypass; on-chain nonce prevents double execution

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Off-chain vote/delegation tracking inconsistencies; possible relayer confusion
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low to Medium depending on tooling

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Multiple delegateBySig txs failing nonce checks from same signer
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Non-standard v/s values in submitted signatures

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
